Off Calendar Oct-14-2015 Continued To Jan-25-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Eric Geiser, M.D.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Separate Statement Of Undisputed Material Facts; Table Of Contents Of Exhibits; Declaration Of John H. Dodd
Off Calendar Nov-13-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ERIK L. PETERSON OF THE BOSTWICK & PETERSON LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-421-8300 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. JOHN H. DODD OF THE LAW OFFICES OF CRADDICK, CANDLAND & CONTI, TEL. NO. 1-925-838-1100 APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT DR. GEISER.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT ERIC GEISER, M.D.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is taken under submission. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Separate Statement Of Undisputed Material Facts; Table Of Contents Of Exhibits; Declaration Of Lisa T. Ungerer; [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Jul-10-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Blake P. Bostwick, of Bostwick & Peterson, LLP, Tel. 415.421.8300, for plaintiffs;
Lisa T. Ungerer, of Law Offices of Rankin, Sproat, Mires, Reynolds, Shuey & Mintz, Tel. 510.465.3922, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS DAVID KNAPP, M.D., AND WILLIAM MILLER, M.D.'S Motion for Summary Judgment is granted. Defendants met their initial burden establishing that Plaintiffs were not genuinely ignorant of the Defendants' identity at the time the complaint was filed. The complaint alleges specific facts as to the basis for the Doe defendants' liability - delay in ordering a CAT scan. (Complaint, par. 7.) Defendants submit evidence establishing that Plaintiffs were aware that Defendants were the doctors treating the decedent during the relevant time period. (Defendants' Undisputed Material Facts 1-30.) Thus, Defendants have met their initial burden establishing that Plaintiffs did not lack knowledge of Defendants' "connection with the case or with his injuries." (McOwen v. Grossman (2007) 153 Cal.App.4th 937, 942.) Plaintiffs do not raise a triable issue of material fact in opposition. The evidence submitted does not address what Plaintiffs actually knew at the time the complaint was filed. Further, the Court finds this case to be analogous to Dover v. Sadowinski (1983) 147 Cal.App.3d 113. As in Dover, the undisputed evidence establishes that Plaintiffs had actual and contemporaneous knowledge of the identities and roles played by Defendants in caring for the decedent. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Laura Martinez, CSR No. 11332; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Separate Statement Of Undisputed Material Facts; Table Of Contents Of Exhibits; Declaration Of Lisa T. Ungerer; [Proposed] Order

Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
